reasoning: The AAA US Corporate Index effective yield currently stands at roughly 4.50% (as of Oct 24), having declined from 4.64% earlier in October but stabilized recently. Long-term US Treasury yields (the 10-year is at 4.02%) form the base of corporate yields. A 25 bps rate cut by the Federal Reserve on October 29 is almost universally expected and already priced in by markets. Given recent polls expecting the 10-year yield to drift up to 4.10% over the coming months as inflation and debt concerns temper aggressive easing paths, there is a slight upward bias for corporate yields relative to this local low point. However, structural uncertainty grows over time. Following instructions to reflect increasing uncertainty by having probabilities for distant dates move closer to 0.50, the forecasts are set in a smoothly decaying path towards even odds.
